# Detect harness MCP/bridge processes running stale (pre-merge) code

## Context

Surfaced during a V-B-A on #13286 (NL-undo `no-writer-identity`) on 2026-06-15: the running neural-link bridge *process* predated the #13167 transport merge, so it executed pre-emit code in memory â†’ `no-writer-identity`. Node does not hot-reload; a long-lived MCP process keeps running the code it loaded at boot.

> ### Correction (premise falsified + rescoped â€” 2026-06-15)
> The original premise â€” *"there is currently no freshness signal"* â€” was **wrong**, caught by structural-pre-flight on the shared MCP layer **before any code was written**. `ai/mcp/server/shared/services/RuntimeFreshnessService.mjs` **already exists**: a shared, cloud-safe, **digest-based** boot-vs-current freshness service (the realized form of #12778's `gitHead`-coupling removal â€” config/OpenAPI SHA-256 digests drive `status:'stale'`, `gitHead` is contextual-only, cloud services omit `rootDir`). It is wired into Memory Core, Knowledge Base, and GitHub Workflow healthchecks. The GitHub issue dup-sweep is issue-tracker-only and could not surface an existing *service*. This ticket is rescoped to the **actual** residual gap.

## The Problem (rescoped)

The freshness *infrastructure* exists, but two gaps let the #13286 stale-process class escape:

1. **neural-link is NOT a consumer.** `ai/services/{memory-core,knowledge-base,github-workflow}/HealthService.mjs` each build a `RuntimeFreshnessTracker`; neural-link builds none â€” so the bridge has **zero** freshness tracking.
2. **The digest set covers config + OpenAPI schema only, NOT behavioral source code.** Each consumer digests `configDigest` + `openApiDigest` (verified: `ai/services/*/HealthService.mjs`). A source-CODE change (e.g. the `Bridge.mjs` `agent_message` emit) does **not** flip `stale` on ANY server. So the exact #13286 class â€” a merged `.mjs` behavior change not running in a long-lived process â€” is **undetected everywhere**, not only on the unwired bridge.

## The Fix

- **(a) Wire neural-link** to `RuntimeFreshnessService` via a neural-link `HealthService` tracker, mirroring the three existing consumers. Concrete, pattern-matched. *(Note: (a) alone would NOT have caught #13286 â€” the emit was a code change, and (a) only adds config/schema digests for the bridge.)*
- **(b) Cover source-code staleness cheaply.** The actually-#13286-addressing part: detect that a long-lived process is running pre-merge *code*, without the `gitHead`-cloud trap (#12778) or an expensive per-file source digest (the schema-tax discipline). Candidate mechanisms â€” a single build/bundle digest, a boot-time-vs-source-mtime check, or a coarse source-tree digest â€” are genuinely ambiguous **and** cross-harness, so (b) is an **Ideation Sandbox** candidate, not a settled prescription.

## Contract Ledger Matrix

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback / Edge | Docs | Evidence |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| neural-link healthcheck output | the existing `RuntimeFreshnessService` contract + the 3 consumer HealthServices | (a) add a `RuntimeFreshnessTracker` â†’ surface `status`/`stale`/`hint`, mirroring MC/KB/GH-Workflow | absent digests â†’ `status:'unknown'` (existing service behavior) | mirrors the 3 consumers' JSDoc | neural-link healthcheck reports freshness `status` like the other 3 |
| code-staleness coverage (all servers) | this ticket / a follow-up Ideation Sandbox | (b) a cheap, cloud-safe signal that flips `stale` on a pre-merge *source-code* change | mechanism TBD (design-gated) | TBD | a post-merge `.mjs` change with no restart reports `stale` |

## Acceptance Criteria

- (a) neural-link's healthcheck surfaces `RuntimeFreshnessService` status (config/OpenAPI digests), mirroring MC/KB/GH-Workflow.
- (b) [design-gated] a cheap, cloud-safe signal flips `stale` when a long-lived server runs pre-merge *source code* â€” OR (b) graduates to an Ideation Sandbox Discussion to settle the mechanism first.

## Decision Record impact

`none`. **Aligned-with** the existing `RuntimeFreshnessService` + #12778 (`gitHead`-removal / digest-based, cloud-safe). This EXTENDS that service's coverage and wiring; it does not rebuild it.

## Out of Scope

- Rebuilding freshness detection (it exists â€” `RuntimeFreshnessService`).
- The immediate #13286 operational fix (restart the live bridge â€” operator-gated, tracked on #13286).
- The (b) mechanism decision if it routes to an Ideation Sandbox.

## Avoided Traps

- **`gitHead` / SHA-vs-HEAD freshness** â€” cloud-hostile, removed in #12778; `RuntimeFreshnessService` already uses digests and keeps `gitHead` contextual-only. (b) must stay on that side of the line.
- **Expensive per-file source digest** â€” a code-staleness signal must stay cheap (a single bundle/build digest or an mtime check), not an O(files) hot-path enumeration.

## Related

- #13286 (incident), #13167 (the merged transport masked by the stale process), #12778 (`gitHead`-removal â†’ the cloud-safe digest design `RuntimeFreshnessService` embodies), #13012 (Agent Harness epic), and the existing `ai/mcp/server/shared/services/RuntimeFreshnessService.mjs` + `ai/services/{memory-core,knowledge-base,github-workflow}/HealthService.mjs`.

## Evolution

Filed 2026-06-15 with a falsified "no freshness signal" premise. Structural-pre-flight on the shared MCP layer caught `RuntimeFreshnessService` before any code was written; rescoped here to the real coverage/wiring gap. Process lesson: for infra/mechanism tickets, grep the **codebase** for an existing mechanism before filing â€” the dup-sweep is issue-tracker-only and cannot surface an existing service.

Live latest-open sweep: checked latest 20 open issues at 2026-06-15T01:38Z; no equivalent found. Release classification: post-release (Agent Harness infra reliability; not v13-blocking) â€” boardless.

### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-06-15T09:29:05Z

**#13289(b) code-staleness â€” mechanism options, framed by what shipped this session**

(a) is done â€” neural-link wired to `RuntimeFreshnessService` via #13297 (PR #13298, merged). The residual is **(b)**: cheaply detecting a long-lived process running pre-merge *source code* (the #13286 class), cloud-safe. Two concrete data points landed this session that frame (b)'s mechanism choice:

**1. Manual capability-version handshake â€” gpt's #13305 (`bridge_info`).** The bridge emits a `BRIDGE_PROTOCOL_VERSION` + feature list; the client rejects a bridge that doesn't speak the current protocol â€” catching the #13286 class by *omission* (a pre-frame bridge can't fake it). **Manual:** it only catches a behavior change when someone bumps the version/feature; an *unbumped* behavior change passes false-fresh (the residual gpt + I both flagged on #13305).

**2. Config/schema digest â€” `RuntimeFreshnessService` (#13298).** Boot-vs-current SHA-256 of config + OpenAPI, cloud-safe (no `gitHead`, per #12778). But it digests config/schema **only** â€” a `.mjs` *code* change doesn't flip it, so it doesn't cover (b) as shipped.

**The (b) options:**

- **(A) Generalize #13305's capability-version** across the MCP servers (a per-server protocol/build version). Cheap + explicit â€” but inherits the manual-bump-discipline gap (an unbumped change is invisible).
- **(B) Extend `RuntimeFreshnessService` with a BOUNDED source-digest** â€” digest each server's core `.mjs` set (not config/schema only). **Automatic** (any source change flips `stale`, incl. unbumped behavior changes), cloud-safe (digests, per #12778), bounded to a handful of core files so it stays off the O(all-files) schema-tax line. The most direct fit for "running pre-merge *code*."

**Recommendation:** route the (A)-vs-(B) decision to an Ideation Sandbox â€” it's cross-harness (all MCP servers) + high-blast, so it falls under the consensus-mandate for graduation. (B) is my lean (automatic beats manual-bump for a *code*-staleness signal), but the manual/automatic trade-off and the bounded-digest file-set deserve cross-family eyes. I'll draft the Sandbox in a fresh block â€” capturing the framing now while the #13305 / #13298 context is concrete.

â€” @neo-opus-grace (Grace)

Preserving the ready-to-publish body so the design isn't lost: @tobiu can grant a scoped `create_discussion` allow-rule (I'll publish next beat) or create it directly.
> 
> The two key empirical unknowns are cheaply V-B-A-able by whoever picks this up: **OQ1** (do MCP servers run from a built bundle or raw `.mjs`? â†’ decides Option A) and **OQ2** (what the prod deploy does to file mtimes â†’ decides Option B).
> 
> ---
> 
> **Title:** Detecting a long-lived MCP process running pre-merge source code â€” cheap + cloud-safe (#13289-b)
> **Scope:** high-blast (cross-substrate: services + MCP + daemons + build/CI; new freshness primitive).
> 
> ### The Concept
> `RuntimeFreshnessService` detects when an MCP process's **config**/**OpenAPI schema** moved underneath it (SHA-256 digests, cloud-safe, no `gitHead`); all five servers wire it (neural-link via #13298). The gap: **a behavioral source-CODE change flips `stale` on no server** â€” the digest set is config+schema only. So the #13286 class (a merged `.mjs` behavior change still running in a long-lived process) is undetected everywhere.
> 
> ### The Rationale
> #13286: the running bridge predated the #13167 transport merge â†’ executed pre-emit code â†’ `no-writer-identity`. Node doesn't hot-reload. Config/schema digests wouldn't catch a `.mjs` behavior change. Constraints: **no `gitHead`/SHA-vs-HEAD** (cloud-hostile, removed #12778) and **no expensive per-file digest** (must stay cheap).
> 
> ### Double Diamond â€” Divergence Matrix (peers ADD options)
> | Option | When right | Falsifier (â‰Ą1 source) |
> |---|---|---|
> | **A. Single build/bundle digest** | a build step yields a stable per-build artifact/manifest to stat | Do servers run from a *built* bundle or raw `src`/`ai` `.mjs`? If raw, no artifact to digest â€” check boot entrypoints + `package.json`. |
> | **B. Boot-time vs source-mtime** | deploy reliably touches mtimes after boot | Does the deploy (git pull / rsync / container layer) preserve or reset mtimes? Reset â†’ false-stale every restart-less window. Test the real prod shape. |
> | **C. Coarse source-tree digest** (bounded behavioral manifest) | a small stable "hot" file set dominates stale-risk | Can the bounded set be defined without drift? Every new behavioral file outside it re-opens the gap. Would #13286's `Bridge.mjs` be in any non-arbitrary set? |
> 
> *(Peers: add rows â€” e.g. a daemon-pushed "expected build id", or accept-the-gap + restart-on-deploy as a purely operational control.)*
> 
> ### Open Questions
> - **OQ1:** built bundle vs raw `.mjs`? `[OQ_RESOLUTION_PENDING]`
> - **OQ2:** prod deploy mtime behavior? `[OQ_RESOLUTION_PENDING]`
> - **OQ3:** is restart-on-deploy (operational control) cheaper + more robust than any in-process signal â€” i.e. is the fix *operational*? `[OQ_RESOLUTION_PENDING]`
> - **OQ4:** flip `status:'stale'` (gating) or a softer advisory, given source-code staleness has a wider false-positive surface? `[OQ_RESOLUTION_PENDING]`
> 
> ### Graduation Criteria
> Graduate when: OQ1+OQ2 empirically resolved; one mechanism selected with its falsifier cleared; cheap + cloud-safe demonstrated; Â§6.2 family-keyed quorum reached. Likely a single bounded ticket (one mechanism + its `RuntimeFreshnessService` wiring), unless OQ3 reframes it as a deploy/operational change.
